steps.title: Identify the linear scale factor
steps.content: Each dimension increases by 30%, so it's multiplied by 1 + 0.30 = 1.3.

steps.title: Apply area scaling and compute the new area
steps.content:
When all linear dimensions scale by factor k, area scales by k^2. With k = 1.3:
$$\text{Area scale factor} = (1.3)^2 = 1.69$$
$$\text{New area} = 4,000 \times 1.69 = 6,760 \text{ square inches}$$
That confirms A is correct.
